name: Sendbird
ipoReadiness: Low — no new funding since 2021; ~12% ARR growth insufficient for public markets. AI agent pivot (Delight.ai, Voice AI) is the reacceleration thesis.

leadership.name: John S. Kim
leadership.title: Co-Founder & CEO
leadership.background: Previously founded Paprika Lab, a social gaming startup acquired by GREE in 2012. YC alumni. Positioning Sendbird as an 'omnichannel AI agent platform' for enterprise CX.

leadership.name: Brandon Jeon
leadership.title: Co-Founder
leadership.background: Co-founded Sendbird alongside John Kim and Harry Kim.

leadership.name: Harry Kim
leadership.title: Co-Founder
leadership.background: Co-founded Sendbird alongside John Kim and Brandon Jeon.

businessOverview: Sendbird is a cloud communications API and SDK platform enabling developers to embed in-app chat, voice, video, and AI-powered customer engagement into mobile and web applications.
Founded 2013 (YC alum); Series C unicorn at $1.05B (April 2021). ~$40M ARR in 2024 with 12% YoY growth — modest for a unicorn, indicating core chat API business has matured.
Processes 7B+ conversations per month across 4,000+ customer applications and 300M+ monthly active users. Key customers: DoorDash, Match Group, Noom, Yahoo Sports.

2025 strategic pivot to AI: launched Delight.ai (AI concierge with long-term memory, Nov 2025), Voice AI agents in 29 languages (Aug 2025), and signed a 3-year AWS Strategic Collaboration Agreement (Aug 2025).
No new funding since Series C in April 2021 — company appears to be operating toward profitability or exploring quiet liquidity options given 5-year-old unicorn valuation and modest ARR growth.
brief.productModel: Usage-based SaaS API platform. Customers pay based on monthly active users (MAUs) and message volume. Revenue through tiered subscriptions plus consumption overages. Three product layers: (1) Communications APIs — chat, voice, video SDKs; (2) Business Messaging — omnichannel SMS, WhatsApp, push, in-app notifications (launched April 2024, processed 5.6B messages in first 6 months); (3) AI Agents — Delight.ai concierge + Voice AI (2025). AWS Marketplace listing as launch partner for the new AI Agents & Tools category.

recentNews.headline: Sendbird Launches Delight.ai — First Branded AI Concierge with Long-Term Memory Across All Channels
recentNews.whyItMatters: Delight.ai reframes Sendbird from a B2D communications API into an AI CX platform — a category commanding 10-20x+ ARR multiples. Long-term memory and cross-channel continuity is a direct attack on Intercom, Zendesk, and Salesforce Agentforce.

recentNews.headline: Sendbird Signs 3-Year AWS Strategic Collaboration Agreement; Named Launch Partner for AI Agents & Tools Marketplace Category
recentNews.whyItMatters: Multi-year AWS SCA with Amazon Bedrock integration provides meaningful enterprise distribution leverage and co-sell pipeline. AWS Marketplace launch partner status for AI Agents & Tools is a selective designation that accelerates enterprise procurement cycles.

recentNews.headline: Sendbird Launches Voice AI Agents Supporting 29 Languages with Customizable Brand Tone
recentNews.whyItMatters: Voice completes Sendbird's channel stack (chat, SMS, email, voice) — making it a full-stack agentic communications platform competing with Twilio, Five9, and NICE CXone. Supports materially higher ACV per customer.

recentNews.headline: Sendbird Launches Omnipresent Proactive AI Customer Support Agent Across Web, Mobile, SMS, WhatsApp, and Voice
recentNews.whyItMatters: First fully omnichannel AI agent — positions Sendbird in agentic AI/autonomous CX, a high-multiple segment. Direct expansion of TAM from developer API infrastructure into enterprise SaaS software.

recentNews.headline: Sendbird Achieves AWS Generative AI Competency Designation
recentNews.whyItMatters: Selective AWS designation formally validates Sendbird as a preferred partner for enterprises deploying GenAI on AWS — strengthens co-sell pipeline and enterprise procurement credibility.

recentNews.headline: Sendbird Business Messaging Hits 5.6B Messages in 6 Months; Customers Cut Notification Costs 60%
recentNews.whyItMatters: Rapid adoption of the new business messaging SKU validates product-market fit and creates a new monetization layer beyond per-MAU pricing. 60% cost reduction vs. legacy SMS vendors is a compelling enterprise displacement story.

earningsTakeaways.aiCommentary: CEO John Kim has repositioned Sendbird as an 'omnichannel AI agent platform' — pivoting beyond pure chat APIs toward agentic AI that understands intent, holds context, acts autonomously, and escalates to humans. The AI pivot is both strategic and narrative-driven: core chat API growth at 12% YoY is insufficient for a $1B+ unicorn; the AI agent layer is the reacceleration thesis.
earningsTakeaways.demandCommentary: Business Messaging processing 5.6B messages in 6 months post-launch signals strong enterprise demand for the omnichannel notification layer. AWS SCA (Aug 2025) provides co-sell pipeline. Enterprise migration from legacy tools is the primary demand lever; NRR expansion from existing DoorDash/Match/Noom-scale logos is the key near-term growth driver.

keyRisks: Growth deceleration: 12% ARR growth in 2024 is well below expectations for a $1B unicorn — company needs the AI agent pivot to meaningfully inflect revenue within 12-18 months or faces a down-round or distressed exit scenario

No new funding in 5 years: last raise was April 2021; SoftBank Vision Fund 2 and Tiger Global are the largest institutional holders, both under LP pressure to return capital — liquidity timeline is a real question

Commoditization risk: OpenAI's real-time API and LLM-native entrants could commoditize the voice/chat layer that Sendbird currently monetizes — Delight.ai must succeed as a differentiated CX platform, not just an API wrapper

Scale gap vs. Twilio: Twilio is at $5.1B revenue (+14% YoY) vs. Sendbird's $40M ARR — competing for enterprise budgets as an omnichannel messaging platform is structurally difficult against a 100x larger incumbent
Stream competitive pressure: Stream (direct in-app chat API competitor) is gaining on developer experience and price; developer-first markets have low switching costs

Valuation reset risk: $1.05B valuation at 10x ARR set in April 2021 at peak multiples; at current market (5-7x ARR for similar-growth private SaaS), fair value is closer to $200-280M — significant markdown risk in any new round

comps.name: Twilio
comps.note: Largest CPaaS comp at $5.1B FY2025 revenue. Sendbird competes on the in-app SDK layer where Twilio is less focused. Multiple reflects margin pressure from carrier pass-throughs.
comps.source: yfinance, close of 2026-06-03 (recomputed: last close × shares + net debt)

comps.name: Agora
comps.note: Closest public pure-play real-time comms API. $141M revenue, profitable. Video/audio SDK focus. Much smaller scale than Twilio — better direct read-through for Sendbird.
comps.source: yfinance, close of 2026-06-03 (recomputed: last close × shares + net debt)

comps.name: Stream (GetStream.io)
comps.note: Most direct competitor in in-app chat/activity feed APIs. $38M Series B (2021). Competes on developer experience and pricing. No public financials.

comps.name: PubNub (Vonage/Ericsson)
comps.note: Former standalone real-time messaging API; acquired by Vonage (then Ericsson $6.2B). Its developer community is a key acquisition target for Sendbird and Stream.

slideBullets: Sendbird processes 7B+ conversations/month across 4,000+ apps and 300M+ MAUs — the communications infrastructure layer for marquee consumer apps including DoorDash, Match Group, Noom, and Yahoo Sports

Core chat API ARR grew only 12% YoY to ~$40M in 2024 — below unicorn expectations — triggering a deliberate pivot to AI agent orchestration with Delight.ai (Nov 2025), Voice AI in 29 languages (Aug 2025), and a 3-year AWS SCA (Aug 2025)

Delight.ai reframes Sendbird from B2D API infrastructure into an AI CX platform competing with Intercom, Zendesk, and Salesforce Agentforce — a category commanding 10-20x+ ARR multiples vs. 5-7x for communications APIs

AWS Generative AI Competency designation and Marketplace launch partner status for AI Agents & Tools provides enterprise co-sell distribution at scale — a meaningful GTM accelerant for a 300-person company

No new funding since April 2021 ($220M total raised); $1.05B unicorn valuation at ~26x ARR is stale — the AI agent pivot is the reacceleration thesis, but execution in the next 4-6 quarters will determine whether this is a Series D or a strategic exit
diligenceQuestions: What is Sendbird's NRR from its top 20 enterprise logos (DoorDash, Match, Noom) — specifically, are these customers expanding into Business Messaging and Delight.ai, or is usage flat? NRR expansion from existing logos is the most credible near-term reacceleration lever.

How is Delight.ai priced and what is the average ACV uplift vs. the base chat API subscription? If the AI concierge layer can 3-5x ACV per enterprise customer, the $40M ARR base can re-rate quickly — but if it's an add-on with modest uptake, the growth thesis doesn't hold.

What are the founder and investor liquidity timelines — specifically, SoftBank Vision Fund 2 and Tiger Global's appetite for a new round at a reset valuation vs. a strategic sale? Five years post-Series C with modest growth creates real pressure to find a path to liquidity in 2025-2026.
